1	/**
2	 * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
3	 *
4	 * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
5	 * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
6	 * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
7	 *
8	 * Routes:
9	 *   GET /          → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
10	 *   GET /sd        → today's APOD image (standard res)
11	 *   GET /optimized → optimized image (≤1200px, auto WebP/AVIF)
12	 *   GET /thumb     → tiny thumbnail (32px, blurred WebP)
13	 *   GET /info      → JSON metadata (includes image dimensions)
14	 *   GET /about     → plain-text description and attribution
15	 */
16	
17	export interface Env {
18	  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
19	}
20	
21	const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
22	const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
23	const CACHE_TTL = 21600;      // 6 hours — APOD changes once/day
24	const STALE_CACHE_TTL = 86400; // 24 hours — fallback when NASA API is down/rate-limited
25	
26	interface ApodResponse {
27	  date: string;
28	  title: string;
29	  explanation: string;
30	  url: string;
31	  hdurl?: string;
32	  media_type: "image" | "video";
33	  copyright?: string;
34	}
35	
36	class NasaApiError extends Error {
37	  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
38	    super(message);
39	  }
40	}
41	
42	const inflightApod = new Map<string, Promise<ApodResponse>>();
43	
44	function apodCacheUrl(key: string): string {
45	  return `https://api.nasapicture.com/_cache/apod/${key}`;
46	}
47	
48	function todayET(): string {
49	  return new Date().toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" });
50	}
51	
52	async function fetchApod(log: string[], apiKey: string, date?: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
53	  const resolvedDate = date ?? todayET();
54	  const cacheKey = resolvedDate;
55	
56	  const cache = caches.default;
57	  const cacheReq = new Request(apodCacheUrl(cacheKey));
58	  const staleReq = new Request(apodCacheUrl(`${cacheKey}:stale`));
59	
60	  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
61	  if (cached) {
62	    log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → cache HIT`);
63	    return cached.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
64	  }
65	
66	  // Deduplicate concurrent requests for the same date
67	  const inflight = inflightApod.get(cacheKey);
68	  if (inflight) {
69	    log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → coalesced with in-flight request`);
70	    return inflight;
71	  }
72	
73	  const promise = fetchApodFromNasa(log, apiKey, resolvedDate, cacheKey, cacheReq, staleReq);
74	  inflightApod.set(cacheKey, promise);
75	  try {
76	    return await promise;
77	  } finally {
78	    inflightApod.delete(cacheKey);
79	  }
80	}
81	
82	async function fetchApodFromNasa(
83	  log: string[], apiKey: string, resolvedDate: string, cacheKey: string,
84	  cacheReq: Request, staleReq: Request,
85	): Promise<ApodResponse> {
86	  const cache = caches.default;
87	  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${resolvedDate}`;
88	
89	  const res = await fetch(url);
90	
91	  const cfCache = res.headers.get("cf-cache-status") ?? "none";
92	  log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → ${res.status} (cf-cache: ${cfCache})`);
93	
94	  if (!res.ok) {
95	    const body = await res.text();
96	    log.push(`  [fetchApod] body=${body}`);
97	
98	    // Try stale cache before giving up
99	    const stale = await cache.match(staleReq);
100	    if (stale) {
101	      log.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → stale cache fallback`);
102	      return stale.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
103	    }
104	
105	    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
106	      res.status === 429
107	        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
108	        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
109	    );
110	  }
111	
112	  const apod = await res.json() as ApodResponse;
113	  const body = JSON.stringify(apod);
114	  const cacheHeaders = { "Content-Type": "application/json", "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}` };
115	  const staleHeaders = { "Content-Type": "application/json", "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${STALE_CACHE_TTL}` };
116	  await Promise.all([
117	    cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(body, { headers: cacheHeaders })),
118	    cache.put(staleReq, new Response(body, { headers: staleHeaders })),
119	  ]);
120	  return apod;
121	}
122	
123	async function findLatestImage(log: string[], apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
124	  let videoStreak = 0;
125	
126	  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
127	    const d = new Date();
128	    d.setDate(d.getDate() - i);
129	    const date = d.toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" });
130	
131	    const apod = await fetchApod(log, apiKey, date);
132	    if (apod.media_type === "image") return apod;
133	    videoStreak++;
134	  }
135	
136	  throw new Error(
137	    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
138	  );
139	}
140	
141	function validateImageUrl(url: string): void {
142	  const parsed = new URL(url);
143	  if (parsed.protocol !== "https:") {
144	    throw new Error(`Blocked image fetch: non-HTTPS scheme (${parsed.protocol})`);
145	  }
146	  if (!parsed.hostname.endsWith(".nasa.gov")) {
147	    throw new Error(`Blocked image fetch: unexpected host (${parsed.hostname})`);
148	  }
149	}
150	
151	async function fetchImageCached(log: string[], url: string): Promise<Response> {
152	  validateImageUrl(url);
153	
154	  const cache = caches.default;
155	  const cacheReq = new Request(`https://api.nasapicture.com/_cache/image/${encodeURIComponent(url)}`);
156	  const staleReq = new Request(`https://api.nasapicture.com/_cache/image/${encodeURIComponent(url)}:stale`);
157	
158	  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
159	  if (cached) {
160	    log.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → cache HIT`);
161	    return cached;
162	  }
163	
164	  const res = await fetch(url);
165	
166	  log.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → ${res.status}`);
167	
168	  if (!res.ok) {
169	    // Try stale cache before giving up
170	    const stale = await cache.match(staleReq);
171	    if (stale) {
172	      log.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → stale cache fallback`);
173	      return stale;
174	    }
175	    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
176	  }
177	
178	  const contentType = res.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
179	  const contentLength = res.headers.get("Content-Length");
180	  const cacheHeaders: Record<string, string> = {
181	    "Content-Type": contentType,
182	    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
183	  };
184	  if (contentLength) cacheHeaders["Content-Length"] = contentLength;
185	
186	  const staleCacheHeaders = { ...cacheHeaders, "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${STALE_CACHE_TTL}` };
187	
188	  const arrayBuffer = await res.arrayBuffer();
189	  await Promise.all([
190	    cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(arrayBuffer, { headers: cacheHeaders })),
191	    cache.put(staleReq, new Response(arrayBuffer, { headers: staleCacheHeaders })),
192	  ]);
193	  return new Response(arrayBuffer, { headers: cacheHeaders });
194	}
195	
196	interface ImageDimensions {
197	  width: number;
198	  height: number;
199	}
200	
201	async function fetchImageDimensions(log: string[], imageUrl: string): Promise<ImageDimensions | null> {
202	  validateImageUrl(imageUrl);
203	
204	  const cache = caches.default;
205	  const cacheKey = `https://api.nasapicture.com/_cache/dimensions/${encodeURIComponent(imageUrl)}`;
206	  const cacheReq = new Request(cacheKey);
207	  const staleReq = new Request(`${cacheKey}:stale`);
208	
209	  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
210	  if (cached) {
211	    log.push(`  [dimensions] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → cache HIT`);
212	    return cached.json() as Promise<ImageDimensions>;
213	  }
214	
215	  try {
216	    const res = await fetch(imageUrl, {
217	      cf: { image: { format: "json" } },
218	    });
219	
220	    if (!res.ok) {
221	      const stale = await cache.match(staleReq);
222	      if (stale) {
223	        log.push(`  [dimensions] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → stale cache fallback`);
224	        return stale.json() as Promise<ImageDimensions>;
225	      }
226	      log.push(`  [dimensions] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → ${res.status} (failed)`);
227	      return null;
228	    }
229	
230	    const info = await res.json() as { original: { width: number; height: number } };
231	    const dimensions: ImageDimensions = {

235	
236	    log.push(`  [dimensions] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → ${dimensions.width}×${dimensions.height}`);

238	    const body = JSON.stringify(dimensions);

242	      })),
243	      cache.put(staleReq, new Response(body, {
244	        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${STALE_CACHE_TTL}` },
245	      })),
246	    ]);
247	    return dimensions;
248	  } catch (err) {
249	    log.push(`  [dimensions] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → error: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : "unknown"}`);
250	    const stale = await cache.match(staleReq);

254	}
255	
256	function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
257	  return {
258	    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
259	    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
260	    "Link": [
261	      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
262	      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
263	      `<${origin}/optimized>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="Optimized image"`,
264	      `<${origin}/thumb>; rel="alternate"; type="image/webp"; title="Thumbnail"`,
265	      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
266	      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
267	    ].join(", "),
268	    ...headers,
269	  };
270	}
271	
272	export default {
273	  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
274	    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
275	      return new Response(null, {
276	        headers: {
277	          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
278	          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, HEAD, OPTIONS",
279	          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",

282	    }
283	
284	    if (request.method !== "GET" && request.method !== "HEAD") {

287	        { status: 405, headers: { "Allow": "GET, HEAD, OPTIONS", "Content-Type": "application/json" } },
288	      );
289	    }
290	
291	    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
292	    const { pathname } = reqUrl;

298	
299	    try {

307	      if (hdPaths.includes(pathname) || sdPaths.includes(pathname)) {

313	        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(log, imageUrl);

315	
316	        res = new Response(upstream.body, {
317	          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),

319	      } else if (optimizedPaths.includes(pathname)) {

467	        res = new Response(
468	          JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /optimized, /thumb, /info, /about" }),
469	          { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
470	        );
471	      }
472	    } catch (err) {
473	      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
474	      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
475	      res = new Response(
476	        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
477	        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
478	      );
479	    }
480	
481	    console.log(`────────────────────────────────────────`);
482	    console.log(`${request.method} ${pathname} → ${res.status} (${Date.now() - start}ms)`);
483	    for (const line of log) console.log(line);
484	
485	    if (request.method === "HEAD") {
486	      return new Response(null, { status: res.status, headers: res.headers });
487	    }
488	    return res;
489	  },
490	};

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

# HTML scraping fallback for NASA API outages

## Context

On 2026-04-08, NASA's APOD API (`api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod`) returned 500 all day while the actual APOD webpage (`apod.nasa.gov/apod/`) served normally. With no traffic to keep the Worker's stale cache warm, the 24h stale cache expired and the site was down the entire day.

## Idea

Add a fallback in `fetchApodFromNasa` that scrapes the APOD HTML page when:

1. The NASA API returns a non-200 response
2. The stale cache is also empty

Scrape `https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/` to extract:
- Image URL (from the main `<img>` tag or `<a>` wrapping it)
- Title, explanation, date, copyright (from page structure)

This would make the proxy resilient to API-only outages, which appear to happen while the static APOD page stays up.

## Considerations

- The APOD HTML page structure is old-school (table-based layout), so parsing is fragile but stable (hasn't changed in years)
- Only triggered as a last resort (API fail + no stale cache), so scraping isn't the normal path
- Should still populate the cache so subsequent requests are fast

```

# HTML Structure Analysis

**1) Main Image URL Reference:**
```html
<a href="image/2604/earthset_original.jpg">
  <IMG SRC="image/2604/earthset_700.jpg" alt="...">
</a>
```
The image is wrapped in an anchor tag linking to the original; the IMG tag uses the 700px version.

**2) Title Location:**
```html
<title>APOD: 2026 April 8  Earthset</title>
```
Also appears as a centered heading:
```html
<center>
  <b>Earthset</b>
</center>
```

**3) Explanation Text:**
```html
<b>Explanation:</b>
<q>And to all of you down there on Earth...</q>
```
The explanation uses `<q>` tags for quotes and standard paragraph `<p>` tags for body content.

**4) Date:**
```html
<p> 2026 April 8 <br>
```
Appears as plain text within a paragraph before the image.

**5) Copyright/Credit Info:**
```html
<b>Image Credit:</b> NASA <br>
<b>Text:</b> <a href="...">Keighley Rockcliffe</a>
```
Located in a centered section below the image, with linked author names and institutional affiliations.

```md
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-08
---

# Automate daily cache warming after APOD rollover

## Problem

The Worker relies on visitor traffic to populate its edge cache. With low traffic (new site), there may be no requests for hours after the daily APOD rollover at midnight ET. This means:

- The first visitor of the day gets a slow, uncached response
- If NASA's API is down and stale cache has expired (24h TTL), there's nothing to serve at all (exactly what happened on 2026-04-08)

## Idea

Automatically hit the Worker's endpoints shortly after midnight ET each day to warm the cache, so the APOD metadata and image bytes are always ready regardless of traffic.

## Options

### 1. Cloudflare Workers Cron Trigger
Add a `scheduled()` handler to the Worker itself. Cloudflare runs it on a cron schedule (e.g., `0 5 * * *` for 05:00 UTC / midnight ET). The handler would call `findLatestImage` and `fetchImageCached` internally to populate the cache. No external service needed.

### 2. GitHub Actions cron
A scheduled workflow that curls the Worker endpoints. Simple but adds an external dependency and GitHub's cron scheduling has up to 15 min of jitter.

### 3. External uptime monitor
Services like UptimeRobot or Cronitor can ping URLs on a schedule. Doubles as uptime monitoring. Adds an external dependency.

## Recommendation

Option 1 (Cron Trigger) is the cleanest, lives in the same codebase, and runs on Cloudflare's infrastructure with no external dependencies. The `scheduled()` handler could warm `/info`, `/` (HD image), and `/thumb` in one pass.

## Considerations

- APOD rollover is midnight ET, but NASA sometimes publishes late. A small delay (e.g., 00:30 ET / 05:30 UTC) or a retry at 01:00 ET could help.
- Cron Triggers run in a single colo, so cache warming only covers that edge. First visitors from other regions still get a cold cache, but the API response itself would be cached.
- Related to NOTE-0022 (HTML scraping fallback): both address resilience, but serve different purposes. Cache warming prevents cold starts; scraping handles API outages.

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/board/planning/NOTE-0023_automated-daily-cache-warming.md
```

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-08
---

# Add annotated image endpoint with title and explanation

## Idea

A new endpoint (e.g., `/annotated` or `/card`) that composites the APOD title and explanation text onto the image itself. For people who want context with the photo, not just the photo alone. Useful as a wallpaper with built-in caption, a shareable card, or a daily briefing image.

## Related

- NOTE-0008 (generate daily OG image) is similar in that both composite text onto the APOD image, but OG serves social crawler previews at specific dimensions. This endpoint is user-facing and could have different layout/sizing goals. Implementation could share the same rendering pipeline.

## Open questions

- What rendering approach? Cloudflare Workers have no canvas/DOM, so options include:
  - **Cloudflare Image Transformations `draw` overlay**: can composite images but not arbitrary text
  - **SVG foreignObject rendered to image**: build an SVG with embedded text, render via cf image transforms
  - **External service (e.g., Satori/resvg-wasm)**: generate an image from JSX/HTML in the Worker using WASM. Satori is what Vercel's OG image generation uses. Would need to bundle the WASM into the Worker.
  - **Pre-rendered via R2**: generate the image externally (GitHub Action, cron job) and store in R2
- How much explanation text to include? Full explanation can be several paragraphs. Truncation or a short summary might work better.
- Layout: text overlay on the image (risk of readability), or extend the canvas with a text panel below/beside?
- Should this share infrastructure with NOTE-0008 (OG image)?

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/board/planning/NOTE-0024_annotated-image-endpoint.md
```
</details></tool-use>

---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-08
---

# Add fallback state for landing page when no image is available

## Problem

When the API is down and all safeguards fail (no cache, no scrape fallback), the landing page shows a completely black void where the image should be. No indication anything went wrong.

## Tiered fallback strategy

### Tier 1: Yesterday's image (future, requires R2)
Once TASK-0011 (R2 storage layer) is implemented, the landing page could fall back to the most recent archived image. Should include a visible disclaimer like "Showing yesterday's image — today's isn't available yet" so visitors aren't misled.

### Tier 2: Worst-case placeholder (implement now)
A styled placeholder that communicates the failure gracefully. Proposal:
- 768px container with a subtle silver/gray border
- Centered message: "Today's image isn't available right now"
- Brief subtext: "NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day updates daily — check back soon"
- Fits the existing dark space aesthetic (not a jarring white error box)

## Implementation

The landing page JS already fetches `/info` and handles the image load. The fallback triggers when:
- `/info` returns an error response (has `error` field instead of `url`)
- Or the image itself fails to load (`onerror` on the `<img>`)

Instead of leaving a black gap, inject the placeholder element into the image container.

## Related

- NOTE-0022: HTML scraping fallback (API-side resilience)
- NOTE-0023: Automated cache warming (prevents cold cache)
- TASK-0011: R2 storage layer (enables Tier 1 fallback)

```
